title: Bogoria ramuana
taxonomy: Bogoria ramuana is a species of orchid native to New Guinea. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This family comprises of over 30,000 known species of orchids. Orchidaceae is a large and diverse family of flowering plants. The classification of Bogoria ramuana reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.
uses: Bogoria ramuana, a species of orchid native to Papua New Guinea, is reported to have several medicinal uses. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful for treating various health issues. In traditional medicine, it is used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The extracts of the plant are also used in the treatment of wounds and injuries. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of Bogoria ramuana.

preparations: Bogoria ramuana, also known as the Ramu bogan vine, is a plant species native to Papua New Guinea. The plant is reported to have antimalarial properties and has been used to treat malaria and other diseases.
